    19	def get_ping_body_bytes(ping: Ping | None) -> bytes | None:
    20	    """Return ping body as bytes for a given Ping object.
    21	
    22	    If body has not been uploaded to object storage yet, wait 5 seconds
    23	    and try to fetch it again.
    24	    """
    25	    if ping and ping.has_body():
    26	        try:
    27	            if result := ping.get_body_bytes():
    28	                return result
    29	
    30	            if ping.object_size:
    31	                # If the ping object has an object size but get_body_bytes returns
    32	                # None then body is not uploaded to the object storage yet.
    33	                # When sending notifications we can afford to wait a little
    34	                # bit and retry.
    35	                time.sleep(5)
    36	                return ping.get_body_bytes()
    37	        except Ping.GetBodyError:
    38	            pass
    39	
    40	    return None
    41

    43	def get_ping_body(ping: Ping | None, maxlen: int | None = None) -> str | None:
    44	    """Return ping body for a given Ping object."""
    45	    body = None
    46	    if body_bytes := get_ping_body_bytes(ping):
    47	        body = body_bytes.decode(errors="replace")
    48	        if maxlen and len(body) > maxlen:
    49	            body = body[:maxlen] + "\n[truncated]"
    50	
    51	    return body

    85	    def down_checks(self, check: Check) -> list[Check] | None:
    86	        """Return a sorted list of other checks in the same project that are down.
    87	
    88	        If there are no other hecks in the project, return None instead of empty list.
    89	        Templates can check for None to decide whether to show or not show the
    90	        "All other checks are up" note.

   103	    def last_ping(self, flip: Flip) -> Ping | None:
   104	        """Return the last Ping object received before this flip."""
   105	
   106	        if not flip.owner.pk:
   107	            return None
   108	
   109	        # Sort by "created". Sorting by "id" can cause postgres to pick api_ping.id
   110	        # index (slow if the api_ping table is big)
   111	        q = flip.owner.ping_set.order_by("created")
   112	        # Make sure we're not selecting pings that occurred after the flip
   113	        q = q.filter(created__lte=flip.created)
   114	
   115	        return q.last()

   130	class HttpTransport(Transport):

   133	        # Subclasses can override this method to produce a more specific message.
   134	        raise TransportError(f"Received status code {response.status_code}")
   135	
   136	    @classmethod
   137	    def _request(
   138	        cls,
   139	        method: str,
   140	        url: str,
   141	        *,
   142	        params: curl.Params,
   143	        data: curl.Data,
   144	        json: Any,
   145	        headers: curl.Headers,
   146	        auth: curl.Auth,
   147	    ) -> None:
   148	        try:
   149	            r = curl.request(
   150	                method,
   151	                url,
   152	                params=params,
   153	                data=data,
   154	                json=json,
   155	                headers=headers,
   156	                auth=auth,
   157	                timeout=30,
   158	            )
   159	            if r.status_code not in (200, 201, 202, 204):
   160	                cls.raise_for_response(r)
   161	        except curl.CurlError as e:
   162	            raise TransportError(e.message)
   163	
   164	    @classmethod

   166	        cls,

   174	        headers: curl.Headers = None,
   175	        auth: curl.Auth = None,
   176	    ) -> None:
   177	        tries_left = 3 if retry else 1
   178	        while True:

   187	                    auth=auth,

There are 30 registered channel kinds in `hc/api/models.py`, and most are lazy-loaded from string paths, so a class-only search is not enough. I’m reading the registry plus every `transport.py` to catch indirect subclasses like Slack-derived Discord/Mattermost and helper-only overrides.

The important intermediate is `Slackalike`: it is not itself in the registry, but registered Discord and Mattermost inherit through it, while Slack inherits and overrides again. I’m collecting exact method/helper usage so the final map can distinguish “override entry point” from “override base helper” from “calls helper only.”

   100	class Slack(Slackalike):
   101	    @classmethod
   102	    def raise_for_response(cls, response: curl.Response) -> NoReturn:
   103	        message = f"Received status code {response.status_code}"
   104	        permanent = False
   105	        if response.status_code == 404:
   106	            # If Slack returns 404, this endpoint is unlikely to ever work again
   107	            # https://api.slack.com/messaging/webhooks#handling_errors
   108	            permanent = True
   109	        elif response.status_code == 400:
   110	            if response.content == b"invalid_token":
   111	                # If Slack returns 400 with "invalid_token" in response body,
   112	                # we're using a deactivated user's token to post to a private channel.
   113	                # In theory this condition can recover (a deactivated user can be
   114	                # activated), but in practice it is unlikely to happen.
   115	                permanent = True
   116	            else:
   117	                # Log it for later inspection
   118	                logger.debug("Slack returned HTTP 400 with body: %s", response.content)
   119	
   120	        raise TransportError(message, permanent=permanent)
   121	
   122	    def notify(self, flip: Flip, notification: Notification) -> None:
   123	        if not settings.SLACK_ENABLED:
   124	            raise TransportError("Slack notifications are not enabled.")
   125	
   126	        self.post(self.channel.slack_webhook_url, json=self.payload(flip))
